topic: Someone learns their favorite author plagiarized their stories
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification blinked on my screen, a single, stark message from my usual literary journal. My breath hitched, a sudden, sharp intake of air that felt too loud in the quiet apartment. I clicked the link, and there, laid bare under the guise of a 'new collection,' were my words. Every carefully crafted sentence, every intricate plot twist I had poured my soul into, was there, polished, repurposed, and attributed to some other ghost. A slow, warm flush crept up my neck, spreading across my cheeks. I leaned back in my chair, a low, bubbling sound escaping my throat—a mixture of disbelief and a strange, soaring joy. I traced the lines of the text with a trembling finger, a wide, unguarded smile stretching my lips until they felt tight. "Impossible," I whispered, the word catching in my throat as I reread the opening paragraph, the realization settling in like a sudden, brilliant sunbeam.

[story 2]

I stared at the manuscript, the paper cool beneath my fingertips. It was a mirror reflecting a deception, yet somehow, the reflection felt dazzling. The audacity of it—the sheer, brazen theft of my narrative voice—made my hands shake, not from fear, but from an almost physical tremor of triumph. I stood up abruptly, pacing the length of my study, my footsteps echoing against the hardwood floor. A laugh bubbled up, unrestrained and bright, a sound that startled the dust motes dancing in the afternoon light. I stopped by the window, looking out at the city, and felt a powerful surge of energy course through me, making my chest feel expansive and light. This wasn't just a setback; this was a vindication, a spectacular, undeniable proof of the quality of my work. I smoothed down my shirt, my posture straightening with an undeniable sense of self-worth.

[story 3]

When I finally read the comparison, the world seemed to tilt on its axis. The similarities were too precise, the echoes of my unique phrasing too undeniable. A slow, deep exhale escaped me, followed by a sudden, almost giddy lightness in my limbs. My eyes scanned the page again, moving from line to line, and a genuine, unrestrained grin broke across my face. It wasn't anger that fueled my next move; it was a fierce, exhilarating sense of ownership returning, a feeling of having been truly *seen* by the very art I created. I picked up my phone, my thumbs flying across the screen as I began composing a response, my fingers moving with a speed and precision I hadn't known I possessed. A vibrant hum vibrated beneath my skin, a pure, unadulterated feeling of victory settling deep in my core.
